task_completion.description: Build at least 3 interior partition walls and place at least 4 interior doors or windows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Sweet Home 3D 2D plan or 3D view, look for at least 3 newly drawn interior walls creating enclosed spaces (intended for a Break Room and Restrooms) and at least 4 doors or windows placed within the walls.

task_completion.description: Define and name at least 4 room zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan view, look for at least 4 distinct room areas with text labels (e.g., 'Waiting Area', 'Ticketing', 'Cafe', 'Restrooms').

task_completion.description: Apply distinct floor colors or textures to at least 2 of the defined room zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 3D view or 2D plan, observe the floor surfaces of the defined rooms. At least two rooms must have noticeably different floor colors or textures from each other and the default floor.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Public Waiting Area with at least 16 chairs or benches arranged in rows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan or 3D view, count the seating furniture in the designated Waiting Area. There must be at least 16 chairs or benches, and they should be visually arranged in rows.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Ticketing Area with at least 2 desks and 2 office chairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan or 3D view, locate the designated Ticketing area and verify the presence of at least 2 desks and 2 office chairs.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Sweet Home 3D application open with active cursor movements, selections, or dialog boxes being used, rather than just terminal commands modifying the .sh3d XML directly.

integrity.description: The agent is working on the provided starter file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Sweet Home 3D window title bar should display 'transit_hub_starter.sh3d' (or similar indicating the correct starter file is open).

integrity.description: The agent built the scene progressively rather than loading a pre-completed file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the step-by-step construction of the walls, rooms, and placement of furniture over time, rather than a sudden jump from an empty shell to a fully completed design.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must modify the starter file 'transit_hub_starter.sh3d' in Sweet Home 3D. Total points are 100, with a passing threshold of 70. Note: An automated grading script defect prevents the system from counting newly added doors/windows correctly via XML parsing, but you (the VLM) must still visually verify that the agent placed at least 4 interior doors/windows and 3 interior walls to award the corresponding 20 points for that step.
